meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no version control (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no version control. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template for Version Control
content.seo_description: Master version control as a student with our Antigravity project template. Solve coding chaos with this advanced guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop manually copying folders. The Antigravity project template automates initialization, integrating robust version control and environment scaffolding instantly. By leveraging Claude Code, you transform your local directory into a production-grade development environment, ensuring every commit is tracked and your workflow remains professional, scalable, and error-free from day one.
content.intro:
You are staring at a folder named 'project_final_v2_final'. It is the graveyard of ambition—a chaotic mess where code goes to die, overwritten by a stray keystroke or a forgotten merge. For a student, the lack of a proper version control strategy isn't just an inconvenience; it is a glass ceiling preventing you from shipping real-world software.

But the era of manual backups is over. By deploying the Antigravity project template, you bridge the gap between amateur coding and professional engineering. This isn't just about saving files; it's about building a resilient, scalable architecture that treats your project as a professional asset from the very first line of code.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish the root architecture. This ensures your project structure follows industry-standard patterns before a single feature is built.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project scaffold using the Antigravity template for a modern web application, including git initialization and standard .gitignore patterns.

steps.title: Configure Version Control Hooks
steps.description: Automate your commit quality. We use hooks to ensure your code is linted and tested before it hits the repository.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up husky pre-commit hooks to run linting and unit tests automatically to prevent broken code from being committed.

steps.title: Environment Variable Management
steps.description: Secure your credentials. Stop hardcoding keys and move to a secure, version-controlled environment pattern.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure .env.example file and configure the project to use a local environment loader without committing sensitive secrets.

steps.title: Branching Strategy Implementation
steps.description: Learn to manage features separately. This prevents your main production code from breaking while you experiment with new UI features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a feature-branching workflow configuration within the project documentation to ensure isolated development cycles.

steps.title: Automated Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Connect your repository to a cloud provider. Your code should deploy automatically every time you merge to the main branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and tests the project on every push to the main branch.

pro_tips: Always use a 'development' branch for experiments; never push directly to main unless you have a passing test suite.
Master the art of the 'Atomic Commit'—each commit should solve exactly one problem or add one feature.
Use Claude Code to write your commit messages; it captures the 'why' behind the code, not just the 'what'.
Keep your .gitignore file updated; never let node_modules or build artifacts touch your repository history.
Treat your README as a living document; if a feature isn't documented in the README, it doesn't exist in the project.
resources: Git Best Practices for Students: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: After completing this setup, push your repository to GitHub, document your first milestone in the README, and start tackling your first feature ticket using Claude Code to manage your development loop.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the architecture used by senior engineers to ship professional-grade software. The transition from student to builder is defined by the tools you choose to automate your chaos. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
